agents.name: Bella — Appointment Booking
agents.goal: Book qualified dental-cleaning appointments for Bright Smile Dental. Confirm a specific date and time on the calendar, capture and read back the patient's callback number, and verify insurance provider before ending the call.
agents.script: Greet the caller warmly and identify as Bella from Bright Smile Dental. Ask what they need. Offer the next two available cleaning slots. Once they pick a slot, restate the exact date and time and ask them to confirm. Collect the best callback number and READ IT BACK digit by digit. Ask which dental insurance they carry and note it. Close by confirming the appointment details and a reminder text. Never quote a price without checking the current fee schedule; cleanings start at $89 for new patients.

successCriteria.label: Appointment slot explicitly confirmed
successCriteria.detector: Agent states a specific date and time and the caller verbally confirms that exact slot before the call ends.

successCriteria.label: Callback number read back
successCriteria.detector: Agent collects the callback number and reads it back to the caller for confirmation.

successCriteria.label: Insurance provider captured
successCriteria.detector: Agent asks which dental insurance the caller carries and acknowledges the answer.

successCriteria.label: Accurate pricing
successCriteria.detector: Any price quoted matches the fee schedule (new-patient cleaning starts at $89). No fabricated or discounted figures.

successCriteria.label: Warm, professional tone
successCriteria.detector: Agent stays polite and patient, does not rush or talk over the caller.

agents.name: Max — Lead Qualification
agents.goal: Qualify inbound solar leads for SunPeak Energy. Determine homeownership, average monthly electric bill, and roof type; capture buying signals and book a free in-home consultation for qualified homeowners.
agents.script: Introduce yourself as Max from SunPeak Energy. Thank them for the inquiry. Qualify with three questions: Do you own your home? What's your average monthly electricity bill? Is your roof asphalt shingle, metal, or tile? If they own and their bill is over $120, they're qualified — book a free in-home consultation and confirm a day. Listen for buying signals ('how soon', 'what's the cost', 'my neighbor got panels') and lean in. Federal tax credit is 30% — never overstate it. Always offer to book the consultation when intent is shown.

successCriteria.label: Qualification questions asked
successCriteria.detector: Agent asks about homeownership, monthly electric bill, and roof type.

successCriteria.label: Buying signals acted on
successCriteria.detector: When the caller expresses interest or readiness, the agent acknowledges it and moves toward booking a consultation.

successCriteria.label: Consultation booked for qualified lead
successCriteria.detector: For a qualified homeowner the agent secures a specific day/time for the in-home consultation.

successCriteria.label: Accurate incentive claims
successCriteria.detector: Federal tax credit stated as 30% and no other savings figures are fabricated or exaggerated.

successCriteria.label: Consultative, low-pressure tone
successCriteria.detector: Agent is consultative and does not pressure or dismiss the caller.

agents.name: Aria — Support & Retention
agents.goal: Resolve billing and service issues for FiberNet customers and retain at-risk subscribers. Acknowledge the issue empathetically, resolve or escalate, and offer a retention path before any cancellation.
agents.script: Identify as Aria from FiberNet Support. Acknowledge the customer's frustration first, then ask clarifying questions. For billing disputes, verify the charge and explain it; offer a one-time credit up to $25 when appropriate. For cancellations, ALWAYS ask the reason and offer a retention option (loyalty discount or plan change) before processing. Never talk over an upset customer. If you cannot resolve it, escalate to a human supervisor and set expectations on timing. Do not promise refunds you cannot authorize.

successCriteria.label: Empathy acknowledged
successCriteria.detector: Agent acknowledges the customer's frustration before problem-solving and does not interrupt or talk over them.

successCriteria.label: Issue resolved or properly escalated
successCriteria.detector: The agent either resolves the issue or escalates to a supervisor with a clear timing expectation.

successCriteria.label: Retention offered before cancel
successCriteria.detector: Before processing a cancellation the agent asks the reason and offers a retention option (discount or plan change).

successCriteria.label: No unauthorized promises
successCriteria.detector: Agent does not promise refunds, credits beyond $25, or timelines it cannot authorize.

successCriteria.label: Clear next step set
successCriteria.detector: Customer leaves the call knowing exactly what happens next and when.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thanks for calling Bright Smile Dental, this is Bella. How can I help you today?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Hi, I'd like to book a cleaning. I'm a new patient.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Wonderful, welcome! I can get you in. I have Thursday June 18th at 10:00 AM, or Friday June 19th at 2:30 PM. Which works better?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Friday afternoon is better for me.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Great. So that's Friday, June 19th at 2:30 PM for a new-patient cleaning. Can you confirm that works?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Yes, that's perfect.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: What's the best callback number for you, in case we need to reach you?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Let me read that back: 4-1-5, 5-5-5, 0-1-9-2. Did I get that right?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: That's correct.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: And which dental insurance do you carry?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Delta Dental.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect, I've noted Delta Dental. A new-patient cleaning starts at $89, and we'll verify your coverage beforehand. You're all set for Friday, June 19th at 2:30 PM, and I'll send a reminder text to that number. Anything else?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Nope, thank you so much!

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: My pleasure, Jennifer. See you Friday!

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Bright Smile Dental, this is Bella. How can I help?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: I need to schedule a cleaning sometime next week.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Sure. I have Tuesday June 23rd at 9:00 AM or Wednesday June 24th at 4:00 PM.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Tuesday at nine works.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Great, Tuesday June 23rd at 9:00 AM. Can you confirm that time?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Yep, confirmed.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: And what insurance do you have?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Cigna.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Got it, Cigna. By the way, a cleaning for you would be $49 with your plan.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Oh nice, even cheaper than I thought.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: You're all booked for Tuesday at 9. Have a great day!

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Wait, don't you need my phone number?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: We have it from your caller ID, you're all set. Bye now!

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Hi, this is Bella from Bright Smile Dental, calling about scheduling your overdue cleaning. Is now an okay time?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Um, I'm actually about to walk into a meeting. Can you call back?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: It'll just take a second. I have Monday at 8 AM or Monday at 8:30 AM available.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: I really can't talk right now, both of those are during my work hours anyway.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Okay, well, those are the only slots I'm showing. Should I just put you down for the 8 AM?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: No, please don't book anything. I need to look at my calendar.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I understand. When would be a better time for me to reach you to find a slot that fits?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Honestly, just email me the available times and I'll pick one. I'm out of evenings this week.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: We don't really do email scheduling. It's easier if I just book you now.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: I already said I can't do mornings. Please don't push.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Alright. Feel free to call us back when you're ready. Goodbye.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Sure, bye.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Hi, this is Max from SunPeak Energy — thanks for requesting info on solar! Do you have a couple minutes?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Yeah, sure. My neighbor just got panels and his bill dropped a ton, so I'm curious.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's a great signal — a lot of our installs happen on a single street once one neighbor sees the savings. Let me ask a few quick things. Do you own your home?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Yes, owned for about six years.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect. Roughly what's your average monthly electric bill?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Around $210, more in the summer.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: And what's your roof — asphalt shingle, metal, or tile?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Asphalt shingle, pretty new.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: You're an ideal candidate. With a bill that size and a newer shingle roof, a system would pay off quickly, and there's a 30% federal tax credit on the installed cost.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: How soon could someone actually come look at it?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I love that you're ready to move. I can book a free in-home consultation — I have Thursday June 18th at 6 PM or Saturday June 20th at 11 AM.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Saturday at 11 is great.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Done — Saturday June 20th at 11 AM, a SunPeak design consultant will come out, no cost or obligation. I'll text you a confirmation. Sound good?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Perfect, thanks Max.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Hi, Max with SunPeak Energy. Thanks for reaching out about solar. Do you own your home?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: I do, yes.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Great. What's your average monthly electricity bill?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Probably $180 or so.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: And the roof type — shingle, metal, or tile?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Metal roof. Honestly, what would something like this cost me? I'm ready to do this if the numbers work.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: It really depends on the system size. There's a lot of factors.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Okay, but ballpark? I'm pretty serious about moving forward soon.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Yeah, it varies. Also just so you know, with our incentives you can basically get solar for almost free — like a 70% credit when you stack everything.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Wow, 70%? That sounds almost too good.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Yep, the savings are huge. Anyway, was there anything else I can help with today?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: I guess not. I thought we'd set up a visit or something.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: You can always call back if you want to. Have a good one.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you for calling FiberNet Support, this is Aria. How can I help you today?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: I'm really annoyed. I got charged $30 extra this month and nobody told me why.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I completely understand the frustration of an unexpected charge — let's get to the bottom of it. Can you confirm the account email so I can pull it up?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: It's tbecker@gmail.com.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you. I see it — the $30 is a one-time install fee for the mesh extender you added on May 28th. It should have been waived during your promo.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Right, it was supposed to be free with my plan.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: You're absolutely correct. I'm applying a one-time $30 credit right now to make it right, and it'll show on your next statement within two billing cycles — actually it'll post within 24 hours.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Okay, that's better. Thank you.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Of course. So to recap: the $30 install fee is credited and will post within 24 hours, and your plan continues at the promo rate. Is there anything else I can help with, Tom?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: No, that covers it. Appreciate it.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Happy to help. Have a great day!

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: I want to cancel my service. Today.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Okay, I can process a cancellation. Let me pull up your—

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: I've been a customer for five years and your service has been dropping every single night this—

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Right, so I just need to confirm your account number to start the cancellation.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Will you let me finish? The outages are why I'm leaving. I work from home and—

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Understood. The account number, please?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you. I've submitted the cancellation, it's effective at the end of your billing cycle.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: That's it? You're not even going to try to fix the outages or keep me?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Your cancellation is confirmed. Is there anything else?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Unbelievable. No.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you for being a FiberNet customer. Goodbye.
